blocks.text: caught him looking. She didn't say anything. She was good at that — catching things and letting them exist without a frame.
stayed with the water. It was genuinely good and he didn't need to explain that to anyone.
laughed when he said it. A real one. "Yes," she said. "I absolutely am."
let it sit. She was good at that.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Your shoulders are lower than they were this morning.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: That's gravity. I've been sitting down.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You sit down for fourteen hours a day at your desk and your shoulders do not do this. Don't tell me it's gravity.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Why did you actually say yes to this trip? Not the polite answer. Why.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I ask you things all the time. You said no to Kayla's birthday dinner three times. You said no to the Portland conference. You told me no about the film festival and then went home and watched the same two documentaries you always watch. So it wasn't just because I asked.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Like you needed me to say yes. Not asked-me. Needed.
She didn't say anything for a moment. I couldn't tell if I'd said the right thing or the wrong one and I didn't look at her to find out.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I haven't heard you laugh in a while. Not the meeting one. Just actually laugh.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I know you do. I'm saying it's been a while. And I figured — coast, no cases, three days. Maybe it would show up.

blocks.prompt: She noticed something about him that he never says out loud. Now what?

options.label: "Thank you for coming." It costs him a little to say it.
He says nothing. But he doesn't look away from the water either.
"You planned this whole intervention, didn't you." He means it warmly.
